resources.string: Tai_WellnessApp
Meditation
Healthy Recipes
Hydration Alert
Start Exercise
Weekly Goal
Daily Motivation
Nutrition Advice
Check Progress

Healthy Recipe

1. Fruit Salad
A mix of fresh fruits like bananas, apples, and oranges.

2. Grilled Chicken
Low-fat grilled chicken served with vegetables.

3. Vegetable Stir Fry
Carrots, broccoli, and peppers cooked lightly.

4. Oatmeal Breakfast
Oats with milk and fruits.

5. Avocado Toast
Whole grain bread topped with avocado.

6. Boiled Eggs
Rich in protein and easy to prepare.

7. Smoothie Bowl
Blended fruits with yogurt and nuts.

8. Brown Rice and Beans
Healthy and full of fiber.

9. Baked Fish
Fish baked with lemon and spices.

10. Greek Yogurt with Honey
Good for digestion and very nutritious.

Nutrition Advice

1. Fruit Salad
A mix of fresh fruits like bananas, apples, and oranges.

2. Grilled Chicken
Low-fat grilled chicken served with vegetables.

3. Vegetable Stir Fry
Carrots, broccoli, and peppers cooked lightly.

4. Oatmeal Breakfast
Oats with milk and fruits.

5. Avocado Toast
Whole grain bread topped with avocado.

6. Boiled Eggs
Rich in protein and easy to prepare.

7. Smoothie Bowl
Blended fruits with yogurt and nuts.

8. Brown Rice and Beans
Healthy and full of fiber.

9. Baked Fish
Fish baked with lemon and spices.

10. Greek Yogurt with Honey
Good for digestion and very nutritious.

Meditation Content

1. Mindful Breathing
Focus on your breath, inhaling and exhaling slowly to calm your mind.

2. Body Scan Meditation
Gently bring awareness to each part of your body from head to toe.

3. Guided Visualization
Imagine a peaceful place like a beach or forest to relax your thoughts.

4. Loving-Kindness Meditation
Silently repeat phrases wishing well-being for yourself and others.

5. Mantra Meditation
Repeat a calming word or phrase to maintain focus and clarity.

6. Walking Meditation
Walk slowly and focus on each step and movement of your body.

7. Gratitude Meditation
Reflect on things you are thankful for to cultivate positivity.

8. Silent Sitting
Sit quietly and observe your thoughts without judgment.

9. Chakra Meditation
Focus on different energy centers in your body for balance.

10. Progressive Relaxation
Tense and relax each muscle group to release physical stress.

Start Exercise

1. Warm-Up Stretch
Gently stretch your muscles to prepare your body for activity.

2. Jumping Jacks
A simple full-body exercise to increase heart rate.

3. Bodyweight Squats
Strengthen your legs and improve balance.

4. Push-Ups
Build upper body strength using your own weight.

5. Plank Hold
Engage your core and improve stability.

6. Lunges
Work your legs and improve coordination.

7. High Knees
Boost cardio and activate lower body muscles.

8. Mountain Climbers
A great full-body workout for strength and endurance.

9. Cool Down Stretch
Slowly stretch to relax muscles after exercise.

10. Deep Breathing
Calm your body and restore normal breathing.

Check Progress
Fruit Mix Bowl – A refreshing combination of seasonal fruits that provides natural sugars and vitamins. Lean Grilled Chicken – Lightly seasoned chicken cooked without excess oil, perfect for muscle health. Sautéed Vegetables – A colorful mix of lightly cooked veggies that retain nutrients and flavor. Warm Oats Meal – A filling breakfast made with oats, offering long-lasting energy throughout the day. Avocado Bread Slice – Crunchy whole grain bread layered with creamy avocado for healthy fats. Hard-Boiled Eggs – Simple protein-rich eggs that help keep you full and energized. Creamy Fruit Blend – A thick mix of blended fruits, yogurt, and crunchy toppings for added texture. Whole Grain Rice and Legumes – A balanced dish packed with fiber and plant-based protein.☐ Oven-Cooked Fish – Gently baked fish infused with herbs for a heart-friendly meal. Yogurt with Natural Sweetness – Smooth yogurt paired with a touch of honey to support gut health.
Daily Motivation

Start Hydration

1. Morning Glass
Drink a glass of water immediately after waking up to rehydrate.

2. Carry a Bottle
Keep a reusable water bottle with you throughout the day.

3. Sip Regularly
Take small, frequent sips instead of gulping large amounts.

4. Flavor Naturally
Add lemon, cucumber, or mint to make water more refreshing.

5. Eat Your Water
Consume hydrating foods like watermelon, celery, and cucumber.

6. Pre-Meal Glass
Drink water 30 minutes before meals to aid digestion.

7. Post-Activity Refill
Replenish fluids lost through sweat after any physical activity.

8. Monitor Color
Check that your urine is pale yellow, a sign of good hydration.

9. Set Reminders
Use an app or alarm to remind you to drink at regular intervals.

10. Evening Limit
Taper off fluid intake before bed to ensure restful sleep.

Weekly Progress

1. Set Weekly Goals
Define clear and achievable fitness or hydration goals for the week.

2. Track Daily Activity
Record your workouts, steps, or water intake each day.

3. Monitor Consistency
Check how often you stick to your routine throughout the week.

4. Measure Performance
Note improvements in strength, endurance, or energy levels.

5. Check Body Signals
Pay attention to how your body feels—energy, soreness, and recovery.

6. Review Nutrition
Evaluate your eating habits and hydration levels.

7. Celebrate Wins
Acknowledge small achievements to stay motivated.

8. Identify Challenges
Recognize obstacles that affected your progress.

9. Adjust Plan
Modify your routine based on what worked or didn’t.

10. Prepare Next Week
Set new goals and plan ahead for continued progress.

Start Exercise Check Progress

1. Warm-Up First
Begin with light stretches to prepare your body.

2. Start Small
Choose simple exercises to build consistency.

3. Follow Routine
Stick to a structured workout plan.

4. Track Reps
Count repetitions and sets during each exercise.

5. Time Your Workout
Monitor how long you exercise each session.

6. Note Difficulty
Record how challenging each workout feels.

7. Stay Consistent
Exercise regularly to build momentum.

8. Compare Progress
Check improvements over time in strength and stamina.

9. Adjust Intensity
Increase difficulty gradually as you improve.

10. Reflect Improve
Review your progress and refine your routine.

Healthy Recipes

1. Start Simple
Choose easy recipes with few ingredients to build consistency.

2. Use Fresh Ingredients
Incorporate fresh fruits, vegetables, and whole foods.

3. Balance Your Plate
Include protein, healthy fats, and complex carbohydrates.

4. Control Portions
Serve appropriate portions to avoid overeating.

5. Cook at Home
Prepare meals at home to control ingredients and nutrition.

6. Limit Processed Foods
Reduce intake of packaged and highly processed items.

7. Healthy Cooking Methods
Use grilling, steaming, or baking instead of frying.

8. Stay Hydrated
Pair meals with water instead of sugary drinks.

9. Plan Meals
Prepare a weekly meal plan to stay on track.

10. Enjoy Mindfully
Eat slowly and appreciate your meals for better digestion.

Daily Motivation

1. Start with Gratitude
Begin your day by appreciating what you have.

2. Set Daily Goals
Define small, achievable goals for the day.

3. Positive Mindset
Focus on what can go right instead of what can go wrong.

4. Take Action
Start even if you don’t feel ready—progress comes from doing.

5. Stay Consistent
Small efforts every day lead to big results.

6. Embrace Challenges
View obstacles as opportunities to grow.

7. Learn Daily
Gain something new each day, no matter how small.

8. Avoid Negativity
Limit exposure to negative thoughts and influences.

9. Reflect on Progress
Look back at what you’ve achieved, not just what’s left.

10. End with Positivity
Finish your day with a positive thought or reflection.
